D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Indigo Vat — Reduce, Apply, Oxidise

indigo is insoluble blue; reduce it to soluble yellow leuco-indigo, soak the fibre, then air-oxidise back to blue — locked inside the fabric forever

Indigo (the molecule that makes blue jeans) is insoluble in water as the oxidised quinone form. Vat dyeing uses chemistry to fix it: reduce with sodium dithionite to the soluble yellow leuco form, soak the cotton, and oxidise back in air. The reformed indigo crystals are now physically trapped inside the fibre.

Cycle through the three states. Each step shows the form, its λ_max, and the colour of the dye vat / dyed fabric. The historical method (woad fermentation) achieved the same redox cycle biochemically.
